title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with heartburn because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound reduces inflammation in the esophagus, which can cause heartburn symptoms to subside. Additionally, licorice tea also relaxes the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus more easily and reducing discomfort. Overall, herbal licorice teas work to calm and soothe digestive issues, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have antioxidant and anti-irritant effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Licoricidin: This compound has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, which can help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, making it effective in reducing symptoms of heartburn.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried glycyrrhizae root powder into a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Bring one cup of water to a boil and pour it over the powdered root in the teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and active compounds to meld together.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately, ideally 15-20 minutes before meals or as needed to alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with heartburn because its natural oils, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, have a calming effect on the esophagus and reduce inflammation. When consumed as an herbal tea, peppermint relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus more easily, reducing symptoms of heartburn and indigestion. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the lining of the esophagus, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Antacid Properties: Peppermint tea has antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from heartburn symptoms such as burning chest pain and discomfort.

Relaxation of Smooth Muscle: Peppermint's menthol content helps relax the smooth muscle in the esophagus, allowing food to pass through the digestive system more easily and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to heartburn.
Inflammation Reduction: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have been traditionally used to create a calming and soothing tea that can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, have anti-inflammatory properties that make them effective in treating heartburn and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or two teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves.
Heat one cup of water in a tea kettle until it reaches its boiling point for five minutes.
Pour the hot water over the peppermint leaves and let it steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey if desired and drink the tea immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of heartburn such as burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter and reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_properties: Ginger's Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

Digestive Enzyme Stimulation: Ginger has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes in the body, including saliva and stomach acid, which helps break down food more efficiently and reduces the likelihood of acid reflux and heartburn.

Relaxation Properties: Ginger has a calming effect on the smooth muscle in the esophagus, helping to relax and reduce spasms that can contribute to heartburn symptoms, making it easier for food to pass through the digestive tract without discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help soothe and calm the digestive system to alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Roots: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, roots can reduce inflammation and relax the esophageal sphincter to prevent acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 1-inch piece of fresh ginger root, 2 cups boiling water, and honey to taste.
Peel the ginger root using a spoon or vegetable peeler to remove skin and outer layer.
Slice the peeled ginger root into thin coins using a sharp knife or mandoline for even cooking.
Steep the sliced ginger in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, then add honey to taste and serve hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with heartburn because of its natural antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The tea's carminative action also helps to ease digestion and relieve gas and bloating, common symptoms associated with heartburn. Additionally, caraway's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antacid property: Caraway tea has an antacid effect, which helps neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of heartburn by coating the esophagus and relieving inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in caraway tea may help reduce swelling and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort associated with heartburn.

Digestive enzyme stimulant property: Caraway tea has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes, which can aid in proper digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and heartburn by breaking down food more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Roots: Caraway roots contain a significant amount of volatile oils, particularly cuminaldehyde, which helps soothe digestive issues and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Caraway seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to calm stomach upset and reduce inflammation, making them an effective addition to medicinal teas for heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried caraway seeds for every eight ounces of water used.
Rinse the caraway seeds in cold running water then gently rub them between fingers.
Combine the rinsed caraway seeds with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for five to ten minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. The apigenin present in chamomile binds to the alpha receptors in the brain, calming the muscles in the esophagus and reducing inflammation caused by acid reflux. This relaxation also helps to reduce symptoms such as bloating, gas, and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help alleviate stress-related digestive issues that may exacerbate heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with heartburn.

Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin present in chamomile tea binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms; this soothing effect also helps calm the digestive system.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds help relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms and contractions that can cause heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and protect the digestive tract against acid reflux.
Flowers: Known for their calming effects on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from an airtight container.
Measure two cups of boiling water and pour it over the harvested herbs in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and properties to meld together effectively.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids completely.
Consume the tea immediately, sipping slowly as needed to help alleviate heartburn symptoms naturally within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with heartburn because of its natural properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The essential oils present in fennel, such as anethole, have a direct impact on reducing inflammation and relaxing the esophageal muscles, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to ease bloating and gas, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing heartburn discomfort. As a result, drinking herbal fennel tea may provide quick relief from heartburn and related digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tea's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms by easing pressure on the esophageal sphincter.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in fennel tea relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms that can cause acid reflux and heartburn.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms by protecting against damage caused by excess stomach acid.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm heartburn symptoms, reducing inflammation in the esophagus.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds and pour into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop then remove from heat.
Add the fennel seeds to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate cup.
Discard the solids and serve the tea hot or allow it to cool before drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime teas helps with heartburn because of its natural antacid properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The tea's calming effects on the digestive system also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach contents from flowing back up into the throat. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to soothe and protect the lining of the esophagus, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of calm in individuals experiencing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Broad-leaved lime teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of broad-leaved lime tea helps neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and contribute to heartburn, promoting a healthier digestive environment.
Muscle-relaxing compounds: Broad-leaved lime teas contain muscle-relaxing compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which can help relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant contain antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves or dried herb in amounts of one tablespoon for every cup of boiling water.
Bring fresh or dried tilia platyphyllos leaves to a boil in four cups of clean filtered water for five minutes.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before pouring it into individual serving cups and refrigerating for at least two hours.
Drink the chilled tilia platyphyllos tea in moderation three times daily as needed to alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. Additionally, stinging nettle's antacid properties help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain associated with heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and carotenoids that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn by reducing swelling and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to heartburn and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) by damaging the esophageal lining and increasing inflammation.

Soothing mucilages: The mucilage found in stinging nettle tea has a calming effect on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid and reducing irritation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal teas for heartburn because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the stinging nettle plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe heartburn and acid reflux by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and stems or purchase dried nettle herbs from a reputable supplier.
Rinse the dried nettle herbs thoroughly with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried nettle leaves with one cup of boiling water in a large teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water and release their benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy as an anti-inflammatory and antacid remedy to help alleviate heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with heartburn because it contains inulin, a type of fiber that acts as a natural antacid. Inulin absorbs excess stomach acid and gas, reducing inflammation and soothing the esophageal lining. Additionally, chicory tea's bitter compounds stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, preventing acid reflux and heartburn symptoms. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to heal damaged esophageal tissue, providing long-term relief from heartburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Inulin: Chicory tea contains a prebiotic fiber called inulin, which helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in chicory tea, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.

Mucilage: Chicory root contains a thick, protective substance called mucilage, which forms a gel-like coating on the lining of the digestive tract, helping to neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chicory contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Chicory's rhyzome is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help calm the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chicory leaves or purchase dried chicory root from a reputable source, storing it in an airtight container.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chicory root or three tablespoons of fresh chicory leaves per eight ounce cup of boiling water.
Steep the chicory mixture for ten to fifteen minutes, allowing the bitter compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Enjoy the herbal tea immediately, sweetening it with honey if desired, as needed for heartburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with heartburn because its natural ingredients have a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing inflammation and reducing acidity in the esophagus. The tea's diuretic properties also help to reduce bloating and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate digestion can improve the body's ability to absorb nutrients, further alleviating symptoms of heartburn. Overall, dandelion teas provide a gentle and effective remedy for those seeking natural relief from heartburn.
remedy_properties: Antacid effects: Dandelion tea's high concentration of taraxasterol and other triterpenoids helps to neutralize stomach acid, providing fast relief from heartburn symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin in dandelion tea can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and digestive tract, which contributes to heartburn and acid reflux.

Digestive stimulant action: Dandelion root's bitter compounds can stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, dandelion roots help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, dandelion flowers soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Rinse one tablespoon of dried dandelion flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers with filtered water to remove impurities.
Boil one cup of water in a teapot and then steep the rinsed dandelion herbs for 5-7 minutes to extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately after preparation time has elapsed.
Enjoy the warm tea within 30 minutes of brewing and repeat every 2 hours as needed to alleviate heartburn symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for heartburn are a natural remedy that can provide relief from the discomfort of acid reflux and indigestion. These teas work by soothing the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation, and calming digestive enzymes to alleviate symptoms such as burning sensations in the chest and throat. Examples of herbal teas that help with heartburn include chamomile, licorice root, and peppermint, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can improve digestion. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals with heartburn can experience improved sleep quality, reduced discomfort during meals, and increased confidence in their daily lives.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heartburn with herbal teas is to opt for calming and soothing options that neutralize stomach acid. Peppermint tea is a popular choice, as its menthol content helps relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce inflammation. Ginger tea also provides relief by reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Additionally, chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory properties help calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal teas are gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), acid indigestion, and inflammation of the digestive tract. Teas like chamomile, peppermint, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive issues, while others like ginger and turmeric have natural antacids that help neutralize stomach acids. These teas can provide relief from discomfort, bloating, and pain associated with these conditions without the side effects of medication.
